items.title: Release Planning
items.summary: Craft one target release from selected roadmap items and manually entered ad hoc items, then approve it into Release Truth.

items.title: Release Truth Kanban view
items.summary: Show Release Truth items in a Kanban projection without creating a second task model.

items.title: Data file watching and auto refresh
items.summary: Refresh the local viewer after watched Architext JSON changes validate, while preserving the last known good model on invalid writes.

items.title: Routing correctness pass
items.summary: Improve dense diagram routing readability and add fixtures for remaining Deployment and Data/Risks overlaps.

items.title: Viewer refactor boundaries
items.summary: Extract viewer shell, panels, canvas, diagram renderers, detail presenters, and browser hooks into clearer boundaries.

items.title: CLI lifecycle refactor boundaries
items.summary: Move lifecycle commands out of the executable bootstrap behind explicit use cases and ports.

items.title: CI and release gate hardening
items.summary: Keep releasability verified through repository-owned tests, validation, build, package dry-run, and packed CLI smoke tests.

items.title: PDF export
items.summary: Export the active Architext view as a portable PDF artifact with clear diagnostics.

items.title: Schema and data migrations
items.summary: Define explicit migration commands and dry-run reporting for schema evolution in data-only repositories.

items.title: Source extraction drafts
items.summary: Explore source-file inspection that suggests draft architecture-data changes requiring review.

items.title: Rules section
items.summary: Add a JSON-backed Rules view for architecture and project rules, with editable entries, edit/delete protection, priority-ranked ordering, and protected-aware drag/drop reordering.

items.title: Draft release plan approval
items.summary: Saved draft release plans remain approvable without requiring a new preview-only UI state.

items.title: Workflow diagrams
items.summary: Add workflow-oriented diagrams to the Flows diagram set as a first-class view over ordered work/use-case paths without duplicating routing or renderer logic.

items.title: Summaries should be visible in VIEW TRUTH mode
items.summary: Currently the summaries are only visible during editing

items.title: Replace CLEAR with NOT BLOCKED
items.summary: Release Truth labels incomplete unblocked items as NOT BLOCKED instead of CLEAR so blocker state is explicit.

items.title: Non-destructive data refresh
items.summary: Data watching must not replace in-progress browser edits and must apply valid refreshes atomically only when editing surfaces are clean.

items.title: Preserve release item state on plan extension
items.summary: Adding items to an approved or in-process release plan must preserve completed and in-progress state for existing release items.

items.title: Automated verification harness
items.summary: Create a release-grade automated unit, integration, browser, and UAT harness that exercises core user journeys before publication.

items.title: Rules editor action placement
items.summary: Move newly added rule action buttons below the rule form and use highlighted action colors so save/cancel/delete controls are visually obvious and spatially attached to the form.

items.title: Agent instruction rule migration
items.summary: Offer a doctor/sync migration that extracts project rules from AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, Cursor rule files, and legacy .cursorrules into docs/architext/data/rules.json, then replaces tool-specific files with model-agnostic pointers.
evidence: test/cli.test.mjs doctor and sync migrate model-specific instruction rules into Rules data

items.title: Remember sync choices
items.summary: Persist repository-level sync choices from the first interactive run and provide an explicit forget/reset option so users can rerun sync without repeated prompts while still being able to discard stale choices.

items.title: Serve lifecycle controls
items.summary: Add explicit serve modes for foreground and background execution, browser launch controls, clickable URL output, status inspection, and server stop behavior.

items.title: Repository tree view
items.summary: Add a top-level Repo Tree tab that helps users understand source layout, optionally color-coding folders tied to C4 or Flow responsibilities and leaving room for a future 3D graph projection.

items.title: Repository blast-radius search
items.summary: Provide search for filenames, components, or known code concepts so users can see where a concern reaches across repository files and architecture elements.

items.title: Diagram spacing controls
items.summary: Add explicit ergonomics controls for diagram column width and sequence-diagram row spacing so users can improve readability without relying only on zoom.

items.title: Persistent architecture element notes
items.summary: Allow users to edit or annotate architecture elements and persist the notes in Architext JSON, including cases where a high-risk area is intentionally mitigated by the system being documented.

items.title: Rewrite the CLI and backend in Go (remove Node from the product)
items.summary: Reimplement the architext CLI, schema/reference validator, local serve HTTP server, and lifecycle commands (sync, doctor, migrate, status) in Go, shipped as a single self-contained static binary that embeds the prebuilt viewer assets (go:embed). The driver is eliminating Node and its largely unvettable npm dependency/supply-chain surface from the end product: users would install one signed binary with no Node runtime and no transitive npm packages at runtime. Scope keeps the existing React viewer as-is (served from the embedded build); ships as 1.7.0 (maintainer decision 2026-06-10; targetReleaseId will be set when the v1-7-0 release is created at Release Planning kickoff, since Release Truth ids must reference existing releases) — a minor release is defensible because the JSON data contract and CLI surface are preserved exactly (only the implementation language and distribution change); it must preserve the JSON data contract and exact validation parity (port the ajv schemas + reference checks, with a conformance corpus proving identical accept/reject behavior across the Node and Go validators). Residual to note honestly: building the viewer still uses npm/Vite at build time, so this removes Node from what ships and runs, not from the dev/build toolchain; a later full-viewer rewrite would be a separate, larger initiative. Sequencing: schedule after the 1.6.x feature/design work lands and the data contract is stable, since a contract change mid-port doubles the work. MIGRATION PATH (npm upgrade bridge): existing installs came through npm, so @robotaccomplice/architext must continue to be published — from 1.7.0 onward as a thin launcher whose per-platform Go binaries ship as optionalDependencies (the esbuild/Biome distribution model), so that a plain npm update/install brings every prior-version user onto the Go codebase with no manual step. The npm artifact carries no runtime JS beyond binary resolution/spawn, preserving the supply-chain driver: npm remains only a delivery channel, with native installers (brew/curl/releases) as the recommended path for new users. SUSPENDED (maintainer directive 2026-06-11): 1.7.0 work does not start until the 1.6.x patch stream is exhausted — 1.6.2 ships the live routing-progress overlay, 1.6.3 ships the separateCloseParallelRoutes optimization, and any further wanted patches ship before the rewrite begins.

items.title: Consolidate viewer modes onto the shared design language
items.summary: Consolidate the viewer modes onto the shared design language in viewer/DESIGN.md. DELIVERED: (1) component-type color is now single-sourced from the --c4-* tokens in C4_COLOR (repoTreeColors.js), fixing the worker mismatch (was purple in the views vs yellow in the diagram) and adding --c4-system/--c4-queue tokens + matching .c4-node classes so diagram and views agree for every type; (2) the colored left rail is one custom property --accent across detail/notes/blast (was three names --section-accent/--card-accent/--note-accent); (3) section-label tracking converged onto var(--overline-tracking) for the drifting headers (repo-tree col header, blast section titles, diagram-config headers, kanban headers). DEFERRED (low value / high churn): rewriting each mode's existing markup to use the literal .overline / .accent-surface utility classes (the tokens already give the shared treatment without the churn) and a full chip/count-pill component unification (cosmetically consistent already); revisit only if a future mode needs them. Verified live on the corpus: worker rgb(255,210,31), section-label tracking 0.08em, all accent rails render; 392/392 tests, validate clean.

items.title: Parallelize server-side route calculation
items.summary: The serve-side plan precompute farm (shipped 1.6.3-era, PR #107) computes flow x view plans sequentially in a single worker thread, so a full warmup is the sum of all plan times (~3 minutes for the routing corpus). Now that planning lives in the Node backend, fan the farm out across a worker-thread pool (cpu_count - 1, plans are independent and embarrassingly parallel) to cut warmup roughly by the pool width, prioritizing the default view x flow pairs first so the first screen a user opens is warm earliest. Generation-based invalidation already tolerates concurrent completion; the pool must preserve it. Also a direct fit for the 1.7.0 Go backend, where goroutine fan-out replaces the thread pool.
